content.full_text:
Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Eu

Executive Office
1230 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

FPF C
(916) 445[illegible]371
25 JUN 1990

June 21, 1990

Ms. Margaret Ellison
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: November 1990 General Election Ballot Pamphlet

Dear Margaret:

On February 16, 1990, we discussed the requirements of
Proposition 73 with respect to references in the
California Ballot Pamphlet to "March Fong Eu" and to the
"Office of Secretary of State". The primary election
ballot pamphlet was printed in accord with the guidelines
you provided.

We propose to print the November 1990 General Election
Ballot Pamphlet in the same manner as the primary election
pamphlet. However, in conjunction with the political
Science Department at the University of California at
Davis, the Secretary of State will be including a
questionnaire in the pamphlet to survey voters as to ways
to make the pamphlet more useful to the voters. Voters
will be invited to complete the questionnaire and to
return it in an envelope which the voter must provide and
address. The questionnaire itself will contain no
reference to "March Fong Eu" or to the "Office of the
Secretary of State". However, we are advised that to
ensure maximum participation, it is important that the
questionnaire be returned to an "official" government
agency. We propose, therefore, that the instructions
contained in the questionnaire direct the voter to return
the completed questionnaire to: "Secretary of State, P.
O. Box xxxxx, Sacramento, California 958xx". In this
context, would reference to "Secretary of State"
permissible pursuant to Proposition 73 and regulations
promulgated thereunder?

Your early advice in this regard would be very much
appreciated.

Sincerely,

ANTHONY L. MILLER
Chief Deputy
